notes: Distinguished pagan senator and orator, served as prefect of the city during the late 4th century, known for petitioning Emperor Valentinian II.

docs.title: Letter 4051
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.excerpt: You are a most diligent imitator of the ancients and a most distinguished innovator among the moderns. Your buildings speak of your character, for no one is recognized as careful in architecture unless he is also refined in his own mind.

docs.title: Letter 11001
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.subject_summary: Book 11, Letter I: SENATUI URBIS ROMAE SENATOR PPO
docs.excerpt: … recall the memorable saying of the eloquent Symmachus: "I look with happy expectation upon the growth of his virtue, since I delay to praise its beginnings." Come to my aid, senators!

docs.title: Letter 6
docs.author: Symmachus (Pope)
docs.subject_summary:
Ep. 7: 
Ennodii  episcopi  Tieinensis  ad  Symniachuni  papam.

Ut  boni  imperatoris  instar  certamen  contra  scMsmaticos  s
docs.excerpt: Ennodius, bishop, to the most holy Pope Symmachus. As the good emperor maintains certain boundaries which he never allows to be violated, so the ruler of the church, inspired by divine contemplation, reflects the immovable constancy of God himself.
